title: Claiming SMSF setup costs correctly
description: You can reimburse setup costs from your SMSF – but only in the right way.
keywords: elf-managed super fund SMSF, SMSF setup costs, SMSF establishment costs, SMSF reimbursement, claim SMSF setup costs, SMSF trustee duties, SMSF contributions, SMSF corporate trustee ASIC fees, SMSF capital expenses, setting up an SMSF

content_blocks.text: If you paid your SMSF (self-managed super fund) setup costs personally, you may be able to claim reimbursement from the fund.

items: The SMSF must charge the costs against your benefits.
You should seek reimbursement as soon as the fund has enough cash.
The reimbursement must relate to costs incurred in setting up the fund – not for other services.

content_blocks.text: When done properly, reimbursement isn’t considered a contribution, borrowing or financial assistance. But if you don’t seek reimbursement for set up costs charged to your fund, we treat your payment as a contribution.

Keep in mind that SMSF establishment costs are capital expenses and can’t be claimed as a deduction. You can’t be paid for any trustee duties, even if you set the fund up yourself. If your fund uses a corporate trustee, ASIC registration and annual fees will apply.
For more information on understanding your requirements when you set up your SMSF, visit Setting up an SMSF .
Looking for the latest news for SMSFs? You can stay up to date by visiting our SMSF newsroom and subscribing External Link to our monthly SMSF newsletter.

content_text:
If you paid your SMSF (self-managed super fund) setup costs personally, you may be able to claim reimbursement from the fund.
To do this correctly:
- The SMSF must charge the costs against your benefits.
- You should seek reimbursement as soon as the fund has enough cash.
- The reimbursement must relate to costs incurred in setting up the fund – not for other services.
When done properly, reimbursement isn’t considered a contribution, borrowing or financial assistance. But if you don’t seek reimbursement for set up costs charged to your fund, we treat your payment as a contribution.
Keep in mind that SMSF establishment costs are capital expenses and can’t be claimed as a deduction. You can’t be paid for any trustee duties, even if you set the fund up yourself. If your fund uses a corporate trustee, ASIC registration and annual fees will apply.
For more information on understanding your requirements when you set up your SMSF, visit Setting up an SMSF .
Looking for the latest news for SMSFs? You can stay up to date by visiting our SMSF newsroom and subscribing External Link to our monthly SMSF newsletter.
